routine.title: Library after-school reading club
routine.stage_fit_rationale: School-age children can sustain focus in a structured group setting after school, and a weekly reading club directly feeds Hale's love of books while building literacy and social skills with peers.

routine.title: Community soccer or sports league
routine.stage_fit_rationale: A recreational league is perfectly paced for a school-age child — structured enough to teach teamwork and rules, but low-pressure enough not to overwhelm. Placing it on Saturday keeps weekday evenings free for school recovery.

routine.title: Neighbourhood park and playground
routine.stage_fit_rationale: Unstructured outdoor time on Sunday gives a school-age child a genuine wind-down before the school week, supporting gross-motor development and self-directed play without any parent-managed commitment.

parsed.rationale: The week pairs Hale's two core interests — books on Tuesday, soccer on Saturday — and leaves Sunday open for free outdoor play as a low-key reset before the school week begins. Three items across five days keeps the rhythm light and sustainable.
parsed.notes: The "Teen volunteer or maker program" was left out entirely: its stage_fit is listed as 'teenager', which does not match Hale's current 'child' stage. It may be worth revisiting in a few years. The Neighbourhood park slot is flexible — it can move to any free afternoon if Saturday soccer runs long or Sunday plans change.
